title: How to Create Brand Voice in GoHighLevel — AI Content That Stands Out
description: Learn how to create Brand Voice in GoHighLevel from text or URL. Generate on-brand AI content across emails, funnels, and pages in seconds.
html_content:
Your AI is writing generic, forgettable copy that sounds nothing like your brand.

Every email, landing page, and sales funnel coming out of your marketing tool reads the same—like it was generated by a robot (because it was). Your prospects don't connect with it. Your conversions suffer. And worse, you're competing on the same bland messaging as every other agency and business using the same AI tools.

The solution? Brand Voice in GoHighLevel.

This powerful feature turns your brand's unique tone, values, and messaging into a reusable voice profile that every AI tool on the platform uses automatically. Instead of generic copy, you get consistently on-brand content across emails, funnels, landing pages, blogs, and social media—all without manual editing.

In this guide, I'll show you exactly how to create and deploy a Brand Voice that makes your content instantly recognizable and converts. Whether you're building for clients or your own business, this is the fastest way to scale personalized, on-brand AI content.

Ready to stand out? Let's dive in. And if you want hands-on training on mastering GoHighLevel at this level, check out the HighLevel Bootcamp—it covers everything you need to build and scale with GHL.

📋 In This Guide

  • What is Brand Voice in GoHighLevel?
  • Why Brand Voice Matters for Your AI Content
  • How to Create Brand Voice from Text
  • How to Create Brand Voice from a URL
  • How to Use Your Brand Voice Across the Platform
  • Pro Tips for Maximum Impact

What is Brand Voice in GoHighLevel?

Brand Voice is GoHighLevel's machine learning overlay that sits on top of its AI writing system. Instead of generating random, generic copy, it applies your unique voice profile—the combination of tone, structure, phrasing, and messaging values—to every piece of AI-generated content.

Think of it like this: without Brand Voice, every AI tool on the internet generates the same type of copy. With Brand Voice enabled, GoHighLevel's AI learns what makes your brand different and applies that lens to everything it creates.

The platform can learn your voice in two ways:

  • From Text: Paste a 50-100 word brand description that captures your tone, values, and positioning
  • From a URL: Point GoHighLevel to your website, and it analyzes your existing copy to extract your voice automatically

Both methods take seconds to set up and require zero manual configuration. Once created, your Brand Voice works across emails, SMS, landing pages, sales funnels, blogs, and social media content.

Why Brand Voice Matters for Your AI Content

Here's what happens when you skip Brand Voice:

  • Your emails sound like everyone else's—generic, forgettable, low-converting
  • You waste time editing AI-generated copy to match your brand
  • Clients see inconsistent messaging across different marketing channels
  • You lose the competitive edge that authentic, distinctive copy provides

When you implement Brand Voice properly, the opposite happens.

Your prospect receives an email that instantly feels like it came from you—not a template. Your landing page copy reflects your agency's unique positioning, not generic sales speak. Your social media content builds recognition and authority because it's consistent across every post.

For agencies managing multiple client accounts, Brand Voice is the difference between managing 91 sub-accounts that all blend together and running a cohesive, recognizable portfolio of brands. It scales personalization without scaling your workload.

How to Create Brand Voice from Text

This is the fastest method if you already know your brand messaging cold.

Step 1: Navigate to Brand Voice

In GoHighLevel, go to Settings → Brand Voice (or Search for "Brand Voice" in the command bar). Click "Create New Brand Voice."

Step 2: Choose "Create with Text"

Select the text option. You'll see a text box asking for your brand description.

Step 3: Write Your Brand Description (50-100 words)

This is critical. Don't write a generic mission statement. Write how you want to sound. Examples:

Agency Example: "We speak directly to founders and CMOs who are tired of agency fluff. Our tone is confident, data-driven, and irreverent. We use real numbers, call out industry BS, and make complex strategies feel simple. We're conversational, not corporate. We prove ROI, not promises."

B2B SaaS Example: "We help non-technical founders automate their businesses. Our tone is encouraging, jargon-free, and solution-focused. We acknowledge the pain point without doom-saying. We use everyday language, practical examples, and celebrate small wins. We sound like a helpful expert, not a salesperson."

Step 4: Click "Generate Brand Voice"

GoHighLevel's AI analyzes your text and creates a voice profile instantly. It extracts tone markers, messaging priorities, and stylistic preferences.

Step 5: Review and Refine (Optional)

You'll see the generated voice profile with adjustable sliders for tone, formality, and other parameters. Adjust if needed, but the defaults are usually solid based on your input text.

Step 6: Save Your Brand Voice

Name it clearly (e.g., "Agency Brand Voice - Main" or "Client: ABC Corp Voice"). Click save.

💡 Pro Tip

The better your brand description, the better your Brand Voice performs. Spend 5 minutes writing a clear, personality-driven description. Reference how competitors sound and what makes you different. Vague descriptions lead to mediocre voice profiles.

How to Create Brand Voice from a URL

This method is perfect if you already have polished marketing copy on your website.

Step 1: Navigate to Brand Voice

Settings → Brand Voice → "Create New Brand Voice."

Step 2: Choose "Create with URL"

Select the URL option instead of text.

Step 3: Paste Your Website URL

Enter the URL of a key page—usually your homepage or main sales page. GoHighLevel will crawl the page and analyze the copy.

Step 4: Let the AI Learn

The system analyzes your website's tone, messaging structure, common phrases, and positioning. This takes 10-15 seconds.

Step 5: Review the Generated Voice Profile

GoHighLevel presents the extracted voice with tone adjustments available. Review it against how you actually sound on the site.

Step 6: Save Your Brand Voice

Name it appropriately and save.

💡 Pro Tip

For agencies managing client brands: create one Brand Voice per client using their URL. This ensures consistency across all their marketing without manual intervention. Update it quarterly when client positioning shifts.

How to Use Your Brand Voice Across the Platform

Creating your Brand Voice is step one. Using it consistently is where the real power lives.

In Email Marketing:

When you create an email in GoHighLevel's email builder, select your Brand Voice before generating AI copy. Every subject line, body copy, and CTA will reflect your voice automatically.

In Sales Funnels and Landing Pages:

Use the AI content tool within your funnel builder. Choose your Brand Voice from the dropdown, then generate headlines, body text, and button copy. All output stays on-brand.

In Automations:

When using AI-powered automation steps (like follow-up sequences), your Brand Voice applies to every message in the sequence.

In Blog and Social Content:

GoHighLevel's content calendar and AI writing tools all respect your Brand Voice selection, ensuring your blog posts and social media reflect your unique positioning.

The Key: Always select your Brand Voice before generating content. It's a one-click selection that transforms all output.

Pro Tips for Maximum Impact

1. Create Multiple Brand Voices for Different Audiences

If you target both technical founders and non-technical executives, consider two Brand Voices—one for each audience. Switch between them based on the campaign.

2. Update Your Brand Voice Quarterly

As your positioning evolves, refresh your Brand Voice. Delete the old one and create a new version to stay current.

3. Test Brand Voice Output Early

Generate 3-5 sample emails or headlines using your new Brand Voice before deploying it to a full campaign. Adjust tone sliders if output doesn't match expectations.

4. Combine Brand Voice with Segment-Specific Copy

Your Brand Voice is the foundation. Layer in personalization tokens (prospect name, company, industry) to make it even more relevant.

5. Document Your Voice for Your Team

Share the Brand Voice description with your team so they understand what you're optimizing for when editing or reviewing AI-generated copy.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use the same Brand Voice across multiple client accounts?

Technically yes, but it's not recommended. Each client should have their own Brand Voice to ensure their messaging stays distinct and authentic. If you manage sub-accounts, create one Brand Voice per client using their website or brand description.

How often should I update my Brand Voice?

Update when your brand positioning, target audience, or tone shifts significantly—usually every 6-12 months. If you just rebranded or changed your positioning, update immediately.

What if my generated Brand Voice doesn't match how I want to sound?

Adjust the tone sliders in the Brand Voice settings. You can increase "professionalism," "friendliness," "confidence," etc. If sliders don't fix it, rewrite your brand description to be more specific about what you want to sound like.

Does Brand Voice work with all AI tools in GoHighLevel?

Yes. Any AI writing tool on the platform—email, funnel pages, automations, blogs, social content—respects your Brand Voice selection. Make sure you select it before generating.

Can I delete or rename a Brand Voice?

Yes. You can rename Brand Voices anytime. Deletion is possible, but check that no active campaigns are using it first to avoid breaking automations.
